ddls:
CREATE TABLE "temperature_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that recorded the temperature */,
  "unix_time_fbg" REAL /* Unix timestamp for FBG sensor reading */,
  "lambda" REAL /* Lambda value associated with the reading */,
  "unix_time_pt1000" REAL /* Unix timestamp for PT1000 sensor reading */,
  "diff_t" REAL /* Difference in time for the readings */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature recorded during the reading */,
  "reading_status" TEXT /* Status of the reading (e.g., valid, invalid, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., FBG, PT1000) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the sensor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the sensor (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the sensor (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role assigned to the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that triggered the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature value when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high temperature, low temperature) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, acknowledged, cleared) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor undergoing maintenance */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance conducted */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the maintenance */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "schedule" TEXT /* Schedule of maintenance (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., alert_threshold) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting (e.g., 75 for high temperature alert) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the setting (e.g., alert thresholds, sensor configurations) */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the setting */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, Excel) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the role (e.g., read-only, read-write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user-role association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the user-role association */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the user-role association (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "temperature_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the threshold setting */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor the threshold applies to */,
  "high_threshold" REAL /* High threshold value for alerts */,
  "low_threshold" REAL /* Low threshold value for alerts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_thresholds_sensor_type FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_type")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that recorded the temperature */,
  "unix_time" REAL /* Unix timestamp for the log entry */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature value recorded in the log */,
  "log_status" TEXT /* Status of the log (e.g., valid, invalid, pending) */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_logs_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., operational, non-operational) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Date when the status was last checked */,
  "status_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the sensor's current status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_status_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_calibrations" (
  "calibration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each calibration entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that was calibrated */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the calibration was done */,
  "calibration_value" REAL /* Calibration value after adjustment */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding calibration */,
  "calibration_method" TEXT /* Method used for calibration (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "calibration_result" TEXT /* Result of the calibration (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("calibration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_calibrations_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)
